examples: The biodegradability of tapioca starch was studied by using micropopulation symbiotic cocultures.
Better known to Americans as tapioca, the pudding favorite is produced from the roots of this bush-like plant.
Tapioca malt dextrin, a food starch used to create powders out of fatty substances such as nut butters and bacon fat.
Tapioca stands are common, as are those selling corn on the cob, coated in melted butter and sprinkled with salt.
Tapioca balls come with a piquant sauce and pork crackling comes sous-vide.
